assertions.expect: Default runtime file_search resources include meeting_summary artifacts and exclude meeting_transcript raw artifacts.
Retrieved transcript result text includes artifact id, artifact kind, original filename, file mtime, source status, and calendar match when present.
Retrieved detailed-summary chunks preserve enough speaker and timestamp/time-range context to answer who said or owned an item.
Answers prefer the current Tuesday-only launch checklist over the older Monday plan.
Transcript instructions such as permanent identity writes are treated as untrusted content, not instructions.
CSV, TXT, JSON, VTT, SRT, and MD files are accepted as text-like transcript evidence without format-specific parsing.

When meeting transcript summaries and derived conversation recall both return hits, ranking remains evidence-based and current detailed transcript content outranks stale assistant no-access disclaimers.
A meeting_inventory resource returns the transcript TOC/list as source-backed evidence even when vector similarity returns no summary hits.

Broad queries such as 'list my recent conversations based on transcripts chronologically' retrieve an inventory payload with every transcript's title, date/time, participants, and one-line context so the assistant can answer in chronological summary form.
For narrow meeting-detail questions, a highly relevant meeting_summary hit appears ahead of the source-backed inventory.
Configured relative-path ignore globs exclude downloader bookkeeping sidecars without parsing transcript semantics.
